sample_provenance.batch_lot: Bud'ko/Canfield (Ames Lab) 2001 polycrystalline MgB2 — sealed Ta tube reaction of Mg+B at 950 C

provenance.source_citation: S. L. Bud'ko, C. Petrovic, G. Lapertot, C. E. Cunningham, P. C. Canfield, M.-H. Jung, A. H. Lacerda, 'Magnetoresistivity and Hc2(T) in MgB2', Physical Review B 63, 220503(R) (2001), arXiv:cond-mat/0102413. Discovery paper: Nagamatsu et al., Nature 410, 63 (2001), https://www.nature.com/articles/35065039.

scope_caveats: Digitized from published R(T) figure (Bud'ko et al. arXiv:cond-mat/0102413 Fig. 1); values ±5%.
R_over_R_300K dimensionless ratio used (residual resistivity ratio context); absolute resistivity ~10 microOhm-cm at 300 K depends on packing density.
Order-of-magnitude estimates from a single representative polycrystalline pellet; not lot-tested across reproduction labs (within-paper-only).
Tc value 39 K is the midpoint of the resistive transition (onset ~39.5 K, zero ~38.5 K) consistent with Nagamatsu 2001 discovery.
replication_count reflects extensive literature reproduction of MgB2 Tc=39 K since 2001 (Nagamatsu/Bud'ko/Canfield/Finnemore independent labs).
citations: Nagamatsu, Nakagawa, Muranaka, Zenitani, Akimitsu, Nature 410, 63 (2001), https://doi.org/10.1038/35065039 — MgB2 discovery
Finnemore et al., PRL 86, 2420 (2001), arXiv:cond-mat/0102114 — independent thermodynamic/transport confirmation
